page: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 64 physical cores and 64 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.4 GHz. The processor allocates exactly one thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. It includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the D64ps_v6 demonstrates exceptional multi-core and memory performance. It achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench, as well as in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation using Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, placing in the average tier for single-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and bzip3 decompression. Large language model inference speeds are average for prompt processing across various model sizes, but text generation for small models falls into the lower tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture of the D64ps_v6 offers high resource density and excellent multi-core performance per unit of resource. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that do not require local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration. Given its strong multi-threaded capabilities and high memory bandwidth, this server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, Redis caching layers, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpsv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior throughput for database operations, Redis SET commands, and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to weak. This hardware profile provides high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for software compilation, database management, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 64 dedicated cores and 256 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based virtual machine delivering exceptional multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for scale-out workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz with no hyperthreading
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware, optimized for external Premium Storage
Suited for software compilation, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving
